headlines.text: Trump orders immediate US Navy blockade of Strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks collapse in Islamabad
Vance declares 'no deal' after 21 hours of negotiations with Iran in Pakistan over nuclear demands
Rep. Eric Swalwell hit with sexual misconduct allegations from 4 women as California governor bid implodes
Sabrina Carpenter headlined Coachella 2026 as 'Sabrinawood' then apologized for mocking fan's Arabic zaghrouta cheer
Artemis II crew safely splashes down in Pacific after historic moon mission, traveling farther than any humans in history
Saudi Arabia restores East-West oil pipeline to full capacity following Iranian attacks that cut 700,000 bpd
Judge strikes down 158-year-old Reconstruction-era ban on home distilling of spirits as unconstitutional
Suspect arrested after throwing Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home and threatening offices
Man attacks 3 with machete at Grand Central Terminal, killed by NYPD officers
Iran's internet blackout enters day 44, now the longest nationwide internet shutdown on record in any country

breaking.text: BREAKING: Trump orders US Navy blockade of Strait of Hormuz 'effective immediately' after Iran peace talks fail

stories.hed: Vance Flies Home Empty-Handed as Trump Threatens Full Naval Blockade
stories.dek: After 21 hours of talks in Islamabad, JD Vance announced there was 'no deal' because Iran wouldn't commit to ditching nukes. Trump responded by ordering the US Navy to blockade the Strait of Hormuz 'effective immediately,' calling Iran's toll-collecting 'WORLD EXTORTION' — all caps, naturally.

stories.hed: Saudi Arabia Restores Key Oil Pipeline After Iranian Attacks Slashed Output
stories.dek: Saudi Arabia announced its crucial East-West pipeline — one of only two routes bypassing the Strait of Hormuz — is back to full capacity after Iranian strikes knocked out roughly 700,000 barrels per day last week. Small mercies in a region on fire.

stories.hed: Four Women Accuse Rep. Eric Swalwell of Sexual Misconduct; Democrats Pull Endorsements
stories.dek: California Rep. Eric Swalwell, who was running for governor, is now facing sexual misconduct and assault allegations from four women. Democrats are pulling endorsements faster than a ceasefire falls apart, and Swalwell denies all wrongdoing.

stories.hed: Artemis II Crew Splashes Down After Historic Moon Mission
stories.dek: NASA's four Artemis II astronauts returned safely to Earth after traveling farther from the planet than any humans in history. In a world full of bad news, four people went to the moon and came back — which feels like it deserves more attention.

stories.hed: GOP Rep Warns Iran War 'Isn't What They Ran On' as Midterms Loom
stories.dek: A former RNC official is publicly warning that the Iran conflict is becoming a political liability for Republicans with midterms approaching, as war costs mount and the Strait of Hormuz remains a geopolitical chokehold. Turns out 'major combat operations' is a harder sell at the ballot box than at a rally.

markets.context: Markets dipped Friday as Iran peace talks collapsed and Trump threatened a Hormuz blockade, rattling oil and equity sentiment heading into the weekend.

gas.note: Up $1.14 since the war started — Iran found a way to charge tolls on ships AND your Sunday drive.

popCulture.hed: Sabrina Carpenter Turned Coachella Into 'Sabrinawood' — Then Accidentally Started an Islamophobia Debate
popCulture.dek: Carpenter headlined Coachella 2026 with a vintage Hollywood spectacle featuring Will Ferrell, Susan Sarandon, and Samuel L. Jackson cameos — a genuine pop triumph. Then she heard a fan's traditional Arabic zaghrouta cheer, called it 'weird,' and spent Saturday issuing apologies, learning what a zaghrouta is, and promising to 'welcome all cheers and yodels.'

genZ.definition: A traditional Arabic ululation — a celebratory high-pitched trill used by women across the SWANA region at joyful events — that went massively viral this weekend after Sabrina Carpenter called a fan's zaghrouta 'weird' mid-Coachella set, sparking an Islamophobia backlash and a rushed apology. Suddenly everyone online is either doing it, explaining it, or defending it.
genZ.usage: When Trump announced the Hormuz blockade and Iran said 'we're not opening it,' the entire Arab internet basically sent Washington a zaghrouta in response.

tldr: Iran peace talks in Pakistan collapsed Sunday after 21 hours, with JD Vance declaring no deal on nukes and Trump immediately threatening a full Hormuz blockade — raising the stakes on a 44-day war that has gas above $4 and markets on edge. Meanwhile, Sabrina Carpenter gave the best Coachella set in years, then accidentally went viral for all the wrong reasons.
